title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-08 · 以色列与伊朗冲突升级
summary: 以色列与伊朗相互发射导弹，伊朗瞄准以色列拉马特戴维空军基地，以色列打击伊朗石化厂和防空系统。胡塞武装向以色列发射导弹。黎巴嫩总统指责伊朗利用黎巴嫩作为谈判筹码。特朗普与内塔尼亚胡通话，试图控制局势。油价上涨。菲律宾发生7.8级地震。

paragraphs.speaker: Announcer
paragraphs.en: This is CNN Breaking News!
paragraphs.cn: 这里是CNN突发新闻！

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: We are following this breaking news out of the Middle East. Israel and Iran trading missile attacks, and the fragile ceasefire survive this test. Good morning, everybody, I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with the strikes that have happened in the last few hours. Israel says it has identified a wave of missiles launched from Iran. New video into CNN overnight showing damage inside a home in the West Bank. Now, this follows Israeli strikes on an Iranian petrochemical plant.
paragraphs.cn: 我们正在关注中东地区的突发新闻。以色列和伊朗互相发射导弹，脆弱的停火协议能否经受住这次考验？大家早上好，我是奥蒂·科尼什。我们将从过去几小时发生的袭击开始报道。以色列称已识别出从伊朗发射的一波导弹。CNN连夜获得的新视频显示约旦河西岸一处住宅受损。此前，以色列袭击了伊朗一家石化厂。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: The IDF saying that it hit several targets at that complex. Now, those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump, who called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and had told him to hold off on striking back. As tensions rise in the Middle East, so do oil prices. Both Brent Crude and WTI Crude up this morning. We've got live team coverage throughout the region. And we're going to start in Jerusalem with CNN's Oren Liebermann.
paragraphs.cn: 以色列国防军表示，他们击中了该综合体的多个目标。这些报复性袭击似乎无视了特朗普总统的要求，他曾致电以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡，要求他暂缓反击。随着中东紧张局势升级，油价也随之上涨。布伦特原油和西德克萨斯中质原油今早均上涨。我们在整个地区有现场团队报道。我们将从耶路撒冷开始，连线CNN的奥伦·利伯曼。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oren, first, can you just give us the latest on the strikes, what you've witnessed today?
paragraphs.cn: 奥伦，首先，你能告诉我们关于袭击的最新情况吗？你今天目睹了什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: We have seen and heard several waves of incoming Iranian ballistic missiles since about 10:00 or 11 O'clock at night here local time. So, this has been going on for several hours. There were approximately three barrages in that first wave. And then we have seen a few subsequent waves of incoming Iranian ballistic missiles. According to Israel's military, most of those have been intercepted.
paragraphs.cn: 自当地时间晚上10点或11点左右以来，我们已经看到并听到了几波伊朗弹道导弹的来袭。这已经持续了几个小时。第一波大约有三轮齐射。随后我们又看到了几波伊朗弹道导弹。据以色列军方称，其中大部分已被拦截。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: At first, they said all of those -- but when we followed up after what appeared to be some Iranian video of missiles getting through, they said it only applied to an earlier wave. So, we're waiting to see if there is an update there. Meanwhile, Iran's military said they targeted the Ramat David Air Force Base in northern Israel. Israel has responded with its own attacks, and we have seen those progressively escalate from what Israel's ambassador to the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 起初，他们称所有导弹都被拦截 -- -- 但当我们跟进一些伊朗导弹突破防御的视频后，他们表示这仅适用于较早的一波。因此，我们正在等待是否有更新。与此同时，伊朗军方称他们瞄准了以色列北部的拉马特大卫空军基地。以色列已用自己的袭击进行回应，我们看到这些袭击从以色列驻美大使所说的对地对地导弹和非能源基础设施的打击逐步升级。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: described as attacks on surface-to-surface missiles and non-energy infrastructure to the strike. You mentioned on the petrochemical facility in western Iran. And then just an hour-and-a-half ago or so, the Israeli military said they carried out strikes against what they called strategic defense systems that would open up the skies to the Israeli Air Force if it moves in that direction.
paragraphs.cn: 你提到了伊朗西部的石化设施。大约一个半小时前，以色列军方表示他们袭击了所谓的战略防御系统，这将为以色列空军打开天空，如果它朝那个方向行动的话。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: We saw at the beginning of this war a few months ago that one of the first targets was Iran's surface-to-air missiles, and the idea there is that, if you take those out, then Israel's Air Force has to a large extent, freedom of the skies over Iran. And that would also include the U.S. Air Force should this turn into a joint operation. Obviously, that's something we're watching very closely.
paragraphs.cn: 几个月前这场战争开始时，我们看到首批目标之一是伊朗的地对空导弹，其想法是，如果摧毁这些导弹，以色列空军在很大程度上将拥有伊朗上空的自由。如果这变成联合行动，美国空军也将如此。显然，这是我们正在密切关注的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: Crucially, this very much looks like it's heating up, and on the escalation ladder, it's also worth noting, Audie, that the Houthis in Yemen; an Iranian proxy there, fired at least, one missile towards Israel several hours ago that was intercepted here. We have heard interceptions behind us over Jerusalem. We have seen warnings of incoming missiles in several other areas across the country. Here, as both Israel and Iran have indicated and publicly stated that they are prepared for at least days of this to continue.
paragraphs.cn: 关键的是，这看起来正在升温，在升级阶梯上，还值得注意的是，奥蒂，也门的胡塞武装 -- -- 伊朗的代理人 -- -- 几小时前向以色列发射了至少一枚导弹，但被拦截。我们在耶路撒冷身后听到了拦截声。我们在全国其他几个地区也看到了来袭导弹的警告。目前，以色列和伊朗都表示并公开宣称他们准备让这种情况至少持续数天。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You talked about that escalation ladder, and we have been hearing for weeks about Israel's sort of move into southern Lebanon as it goes after Hezbollah. We have the President of Lebanon here -- I want to play this clip so you can help us understand how these things intersect. Here he is.
paragraphs.cn: 你谈到了升级阶梯，几周来我们一直听到以色列在黎巴嫩南部行动，追击真主党。我们有黎巴嫩总统的发言 -- -- 我想播放这段视频，这样你可以帮助我们理解这些事件如何相互关联。这是他的讲话。

paragraphs.speaker: Joseph Aoun
paragraphs.en: Our people being killed, our people being -- our houses being destroyed. They are using Lebanon as a chip -- bargaining chip in their negotiation with the United States. It's unacceptable.
paragraphs.cn: 我们的人民正在被杀害，我们的房屋正在被摧毁。他们正在把黎巴嫩当作与美国谈判的筹码。这是不可接受的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, he was speaking there about Tehran. Can you talk about their most recent strike in southern Lebanon, why it triggered this escalation?
paragraphs.cn: 他刚才是在谈论德黑兰。你能谈谈他们最近对黎巴嫩南部的袭击吗？为什么这引发了局势升级？

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: Well, Iran said last week that if Israel were to carry out an attack, the capital of Beirut, then Iran would directly attack Israel. And that's what we saw happen over what was a very volatile weekend between Israel and Hezbollah. We saw -- we saw the number of people killed in Israeli strikes in Lebanon, according to the Lebanese Ministry of Public Health, rise above 3,600 since this war began some three months ago.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗上周表示，如果以色列对贝鲁特首都发动袭击，伊朗将直接攻击以色列。这正是我们在以色列与真主党之间那个非常动荡的周末所看到的。根据黎巴嫩公共卫生部的数据，自大约三个月前这场战争开始以来，以色列在黎巴嫩袭击中造成的死亡人数已超过3600人。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: And then, even since the latest U.S.-brokered ceasefire which was just several days ago, we saw Hezbollah carry out an attack on northern Israel. That crossed Israel's red line, and Israel then carried out a strike on the Dahiya neighborhood in Beirut, which is considered a Hezbollah stronghold. Iran then carried out its threat or carried out on its threat launching those ballistic missiles on Sunday night directly at Israel. And that has led us to where we are right now.
paragraphs.cn: 然后，即使在几天前美国斡旋的最新停火协议之后，我们看到真主党对以色列北部发动了袭击。这越过了以色列的红线，以色列随后对贝鲁特的达希耶社区发动了袭击，该社区被认为是真主党的据点。伊朗随后兑现了其威胁，于周日晚间直接向以色列发射了弹道导弹。这导致了我们现在的局面。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: President Joseph Aoun, who was interviewed by our Christiane Amanpour, he made clear that he is trying to exercise Lebanese sovereignty, and that includes getting some sort of control over Iran's proxy, Hezbollah, and trying to find some sort of agreement that will lead to a withdrawal of Israeli forces from southern Lebanon and make Lebanon more whole. The problem is, you have an Israeli government that isn't in a position and doesn't want to withdraw from southern Lebanon now.
paragraphs.cn: 接受克里斯蒂安·阿曼普尔采访的约瑟夫·奥恩总统明确表示，他正在努力行使黎巴嫩主权，包括对伊朗的代理人真主党进行某种控制，并试图找到某种协议，促使以色列军队从黎巴嫩南部撤出，使黎巴嫩更加完整。问题是，以色列政府目前既没有立场也不愿意从黎巴嫩南部撤军。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: You have an Iranian government that sees Hezbollah as its foothold in Lebanon, Hezbollah rejected the latest iteration of the -- of the ceasefire there. And that puts the President Joseph Aoun in a very difficult situation. His Lebanese Armed Forces don't have the power and perhaps don't have the will to carry out any serious action against Hezbollah, even as he has tried to outlaw and ban Hezbollah's military activity. What's crucial here, Audie, is that what happens in Lebanon affects the U.S.-Iran ceasefire.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗政府将真主党视为其在黎巴嫩的立足点，真主党拒绝了最新一轮的停火协议。这让约瑟夫·奥恩总统陷入了非常困难的境地。他的黎巴嫩武装部队没有能力，也许也没有意愿对真主党采取任何严肃行动，尽管他试图取缔和禁止真主党的军事活动。奥迪，关键在于，黎巴嫩发生的事情会影响美国与伊朗的停火。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: Iran knows that. They know that President Donald Trump wants to get to a deal, and they're able to try to use Hezbollah and to some extent, use Lebanon as a bargaining chip to pressure Trump to try to rein in Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when it comes to Lebanon.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗知道这一点。他们知道唐纳德·特朗普总统想要达成协议，因此他们试图利用真主党，并在一定程度上利用黎巴嫩作为筹码，向特朗普施压，以在黎巴嫩问题上约束本雅明·内塔尼亚胡总理。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Is there any chance of doing that? I think a lot of people are looking at these actions and thinking about how the President has spoken to Netanyahu, talked about taking down the temperature. What's the view in Israel about how Trump has been talking?
paragraphs.cn: 有没有可能做到这一点？我想很多人都在关注这些行动，并思考总统是如何与内塔尼亚胡交谈的，谈论如何降温。以色列方面如何看待特朗普的言论？

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: Trump made a number of public statements, or rather, gave a number of interviews to reporters in which he said it is Trump who calls the shots, not Netanyahu, saying he would -- he would urge Israel not to retaliate or delay Israel's retaliation. That's at least, the public part of the messaging.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普发表了一些公开声明，或者更确切地说，接受了一些记者采访，他在采访中表示，是特朗普说了算，而不是内塔尼亚胡，并称他会敦促以色列不要报复或推迟以色列的报复。这至少是公开信息的一部分。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: What we don't really know, and this is the key question for us to have a much better sense of what's happening here is, what was the phone call like between Trump and Netanyahu? It is possible that Trump essentially gave this the green light and said, yes, Israel has to respond in some fashion, but keep that response limited.
paragraphs.cn: 我们真正不知道的是 -- -- 这也是我们更好地了解这里情况的关键问题 -- -- 特朗普和内塔尼亚胡之间的电话内容是什么？有可能特朗普基本上开了绿灯，说，是的，以色列必须以某种方式回应，但要把回应限制在一定范围内。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: It's also possible that Trump told Israel not to respond, and Netanyahu is acting on his own here, though I think that's unlikely, given how much Netanyahu needs Trump to be on his side, and has never sort of publicly criticized him. So, there's a spectrum here of what Trump is allowing of Netanyahu. It's worth remembering here that Trump has forced Netanyahu to end wars in Gaza, Lebanon, and previously in Iran. And I think right now, it's still the expectation that Trump will do that once again.
paragraphs.cn: 还有一种可能是，特朗普告诉以色列不要回应，而内塔尼亚胡自行其是，尽管我认为这不太可能，因为内塔尼亚胡非常需要特朗普的支持，而且从未公开批评过他。所以，特朗普允许内塔尼亚胡做什么，存在一个范围。值得记住的是，特朗普曾迫使内塔尼亚胡结束在加沙、黎巴嫩以及此前在伊朗的战争。我认为目前，人们仍然预期特朗普会再次这样做。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: The question is when and how. For that, Trump needs to essentially get the day started in D.C., so, we can learn more about what's happening behind the scenes. Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 问题在于何时以及如何做。为此，特朗普基本上需要让华盛顿开始新的一天，这样我们才能了解更多幕后情况。奥黛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's right, we're waking up here in Washington, Oren Liebermann talking to us from Jerusalem. Our breaking news coverage continues after the break on CNN THIS MORNING. We're going to talk about under what circumstance would Trump consider bringing U.S. troops home? His answer next. Plus, a deadly quake hits in the Philippines. The urgent search for survivors.
paragraphs.cn: 没错，我们在华盛顿醒来，奥伦·利伯曼从耶路撒冷与我们连线。我们的突发新闻报道将在广告后继续，在《CNN今晨》节目中。我们将讨论特朗普在什么情况下会考虑让美军回国？他的答案稍后。此外，菲律宾发生致命地震，正在紧急搜寻幸存者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Breaking news this morning. Israel and Iran trading strikes in the worst escalation since the ceasefire agreement in April. We just heard from our CNN's Oren Liebermann in Jerusalem, where there are sounds of sirens and explosions after the interception of missiles. A U.S. official told CNN that President Trump urged Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to hold off on retaliating against Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 今早突发新闻。以色列和伊朗互相发动袭击，这是自四月停火协议以来最严重的升级。我们刚刚听到CNN的奥伦·利伯曼在耶路撒冷的报道，拦截导弹后那里响起了警报声和爆炸声。一位美国官员告诉CNN，特朗普总统敦促以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡暂缓对伊朗的报复行动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, joining us to talk more about the military strategy here is retired Air Force Colonel and CNN military analyst Cedric Leighton. Good morning. So, first, I want to talk about this escalation because people may be hearing about the back-and-forth between Israel and Hezbollah for many weeks. Why did this particular round of strikes kick off?
paragraphs.cn: 现在，我们邀请退休空军上校、CNN军事分析师塞德里克·莱顿来进一步讨论军事战略。早上好。首先，我想谈谈这次升级，因为人们可能已经听说了以色列和真主党之间数周的交锋。为什么这一轮袭击会爆发？

paragraphs.speaker: Cedric Leighton
paragraphs.en: Yes, good morning, Audie. The reason this particular round of strikes kicked off was because the Israelis hit the suburb of Beirut, suburb of Dahiya. That's where Hezbollah has its main headquarters. And there are a lot of Hezbollah personnel for lack of a better term, in the Dahiya neighborhood.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，早上好，奥黛。这一轮袭击爆发的原因是以色列袭击了贝鲁特郊区达希耶。那里是真主党的主要总部所在地。达希耶社区有很多真主党人员，恕我直言。

paragraphs.speaker: Cedric Leighton
paragraphs.en: So, the reason the Iranians basically came to the defense of the Hezbollah entities there was because Hezbollah is basically Iran's main proxy in the region, and they need Hezbollah in order not only to keep an eye on Israel, but also to potentially mount attacks on Israel. So, every time that there is destruction to Hezbollah, Iran sees this as not only destruction of Hezbollah, but also of their own forces, even though they are absolutely separate. So, this is, in essence, what's going on.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，伊朗之所以基本上为那里的真主党实体辩护，是因为真主党基本上是伊朗在该地区的主要代理人，伊朗需要真主党不仅是为了监视以色列，还为了可能对以色列发动袭击。因此，每当真主党遭到破坏，伊朗都认为这不仅是对真主党的破坏，也是对他们自身力量的破坏，尽管两者完全独立。这基本上就是正在发生的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cedric Leighton
paragraphs.en: They decided -- the Iranians decided that it was time to respond to the Israeli attacks, and especially one as blatant as the one on Dahiya, in their view. And the reason they did that is because they want the ceasefire to cover not just Iran, but also the Lebanese territory, where the Israelis have conducted repeated actions against Hezbollah.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗人决定 -- -- 他们认为是对以色列袭击作出回应的时候了，尤其是像对达希耶这样在他们看来如此公然的袭击。他们这样做的原因是希望停火不仅涵盖伊朗，也涵盖黎巴嫩领土，因为以色列在那里对真主党采取了反复行动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You talked about proxies, and then this also brings me to the Houthis in Yemen, because there's some reporting there that they were saying they wanted to prevent shipping through the Red Sea, which sounds very familiar to those of us who have been hearing about Iran preventing shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Does this threaten broader conflict? Are we seeing hints of that?
paragraphs.cn: 你提到了代理人，这也让我想到也门的胡塞武装，因为有报道称他们想阻止船只通过红海，这对我们这些一直听说伊朗阻止船只通过霍尔木兹海峡的人来说听起来很熟悉。这是否会威胁到更广泛的冲突？我们是否看到了迹象？

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.en: Yes, there's definitely the possibility that this could widen into a bigger regional conflict, at least. So, on the eastern side of the Arabian Peninsula, you have the Strait of Hormuz, which is the area that we've been talking about for quite some time. And then on the western side, you have what's known as the Bab al- Mandab, and that is that very narrow point between the Red Sea as it empties out into the Indian Ocean. So, this is an area where these are so-called choke points.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这至少有可能扩大为更大的地区冲突。在阿拉伯半岛东侧，有霍尔木兹海峡，这是我们讨论已久的地方。而在西侧，有所谓的曼德海峡，那是红海流入印度洋的一个非常狭窄的点。所以，这些地区就是所谓的咽喉要道。

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.en: And every time those are cut off, that impacts global shipping, global trade. And it means that prices go up, oil becomes scarcer. And that creates the economic issues that we're seeing in a lot of the world right now.
paragraphs.cn: 每次这些通道被切断，都会影响全球航运和全球贸易。这意味着价格上涨，石油变得更加稀缺。这造成了我们目前在世界上许多地方看到的经济问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We hear President Trump framing the U.S. involvement more as precaution, not escalation. I want to play for you something that happened on "NBC's" "Meet the Press". This was before all of this news about this escalation. But here it is.
paragraphs.cn: 我们听到特朗普总统将美国的介入更多地描述为预防措施，而非升级。我想播放一段来自NBC《与媒体见面》节目的内容。这是在所有关于升级的消息传出之前发生的。但就是这样。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: If you're not specifically calling it a war, would you consider sending some of the troops who are stationed there, who were sent because of Operation Epic Fury --
paragraphs.cn: 如果你不明确称之为战争，你会考虑将一些驻扎在那里、因"愤怒史诗行动"而派遣的部队 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: You mean boots on the ground --
paragraphs.cn: 你是指地面部队 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: There at home? No --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: Would you consider sending th em home if it's not technically a war?
paragraphs.cn: 如果严格来说不是战争，你会考虑让他们回国吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: We don't need as many troops. I think you just -- like it's on the -- to err on the side of caution.
paragraphs.cn: 我们不需要那么多部队。我认为你只需要 -- -- 就像在 -- -- 宁可谨慎一些。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: What are we looking at? Is it a kind of carrot and stick? Meaning the U.S. is doing the negotiating, but Israel is leaning hard on the military pressure?
paragraphs.cn: 我们看到了什么？这是一种胡萝卜加大棒吗？意思是美国在进行谈判，而以色列则在施加军事压力？

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.en: Well, potentially so. You know, it's interesting when you look at President Trump's remarks, they -- you know, on the one hand, he's kind of hedging his bets here. He does, you know, admit to the possibility of withdrawing some of the troops that are in the Middle East. And I think what he's referring to, are the troops that came in, in addition to the ones that were already stationed in the Middle East. So, what he doesn't want to do is withdraw completely from the region, which is actually Iran's goal.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，有可能。你知道，当你审视特朗普总统的言论时，这很有趣 -- -- 一方面，他在这里有点两面下注。他确实承认可能撤出部分驻扎在中东的部队。我认为他指的是那些额外派来的部队，而不是已经驻扎在中东的部队。所以，他不想做的是完全撤出该地区，而这实际上是伊朗的目标。

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.en: So, if he withdrew completely from the region, that would satisfy Iran. But it wouldn't protect the Arab Gulf states like Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain. So, that becomes a critical point right here. He, in essence, really can't withdraw if he -- if he doesn't want to show weakness. And that becomes, I think, the critical point right here.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，如果他完全撤出该地区，那将满足伊朗。但这不会保护阿拉伯海湾国家，如沙特阿拉伯、卡塔尔、阿联酋、科威特和巴林。因此，这成为了一个关键点。实际上，如果他不想示弱，他就不能撤军。我认为，这成为了这里的关键点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: One more thing. We know that these strikes started back earlier in the year. They were U.S.-Israeli joint strikes, the U.S. gives a lot of military support, Intelligence support to Israel. So, could what we're seeing right now even happen without U.S. support.
paragraphs.cn: 还有一点。我们知道这些袭击早在今年年初就开始了。它们是以色列和美国的联合袭击，美国向以色列提供了大量军事支持和情报支持。那么，如果没有美国的支持，我们现在看到的这些情况还会发生吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.en: It could. And the reason I think we see limited strikes in the western and central parts of Iran, at least so far, is because the Israelis do have a capability to conduct those strikes on their own. They have their own refueling capability, although it's somewhat limited compared to what the U.S. can offer. Generally-speaking, if the strikes were much more extensive or repeated over a series of days, then that would probably indicate a greater U.S. involvement. There may be some U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 有可能。我认为我们看到伊朗西部和中部地区受到有限打击的原因，至少到目前为止，是因为以色列有能力自行实施这些打击。他们有自己的空中加油能力，尽管与美国能提供的相比有些有限。总的来说，如果打击范围更广或连续几天重复进行，那可能表明美国参与程度更高。也许有一些美国的参与，但绝对有可能这些打击目前是在没有美国直接参与的情况下进行的。

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.en: involvement, but it is absolutely possible that these strikes are occurring without direct U.S. involvement at the moment.
paragraphs.cn: 参与，但绝对有可能这些打击目前是在没有美国直接参与的情况下进行的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that's Colonel Cedric Leighton, thank you for your insight. Appreciate you. After the break on CNN THIS MORNING, our breaking news coverage continues.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，感谢塞德里克·莱顿上校的见解。感谢您。在CNN《今晨》节目之后，我们的突发新闻将继续报道。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: To turn this on Israel, it would be a bit of a joke. Iran is the one that decided to break the truce.
paragraphs.cn: 如果把这归咎于以色列，那就有点可笑了。是伊朗决定打破停火协议的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: President Trump urged Israel not to retaliate. We're going to talk about that more. Plus, Trump's cage fight just days away. Could it get knocked out before round one? There's a new lawsuit looking to stop it.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统敦促以色列不要报复。我们将进一步讨论这个问题。另外，特朗普的笼斗比赛就在几天后。它会在第一轮之前就被取消吗？一项新的诉讼试图阻止它。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's now 23 minutes past the hour. Here are five things making the news this morning. Democratic Senate primary candidate Graham Platner held an event in Maine in front of an enthusiastic and cheering crowd just two days before the primary. He declared he is very much just some random guy from Sullivan, Maine.
paragraphs.cn: 现在是整点过23分。以下是今晨的五条新闻。民主党参议院初选候选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳在初选前两天在缅因州举行了一场活动，现场人群热情欢呼。他宣称自己只是来自缅因州沙利文的一个普通人。

paragraphs.speaker: Graham Platner
paragraphs.en: In a moment like this in history, if we can show that a regular person from the regular world who simply comes out with a message saying that we all have to work together to push back against all of the structures of power that have been exploiting and oppressing us f or generations. If we show that, that works, we get to show this country the way forward.
paragraphs.cn: 在历史上的这样一个时刻，如果我们能证明一个来自普通世界的普通人，仅仅站出来说我们必须共同努力，抵制那些世代剥削和压迫我们的权力结构，如果我们证明这是可行的，我们就能向这个国家展示前进的道路。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Platner is still favored to win Tuesday's primary, despite the "New York Times" publishing a report from three of Platner's ex- girlfriends describing, quote, "volatile and toxic relationships". And in one account, physical intimidation. Platner has denied that. As New York is set to host the NBA finals and President Trump tonight, security concerns have been heightened after six people were hurt in a stabbing at Penn station.
paragraphs.cn: 尽管《纽约时报》发表了一篇报道，称普拉特纳的三位前女友描述了"动荡和有毒的关系"，其中一次还涉及身体恐吓，但普拉特纳仍有望赢得周二的初选。普拉特纳否认了这些指控。由于纽约将举办NBA总决赛，特朗普总统今晚也将出席，在宾州车站发生刺伤事件导致六人受伤后，安全担忧加剧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Officials say one of the victims suffered serious injuries, the others suffered non-life-threatening injuries. The suspect is in custody. And there was a deadly 7.8 magnitude earthquake in the Philippines. Video from the area is showing you here, massive damage, crumbling buildings and several people were killed. Rescues are currently underway. And then in just a few hours, the U.S. special forces soldier charged with betting on the raid to capture Nicolas Maduro will appear in court.
paragraphs.cn: 官员称，其中一名受害者伤势严重，其他人没有生命危险。嫌疑人已被拘留。菲律宾发生7.8级致命地震。来自该地区的视频显示，巨大破坏、建筑倒塌，数人死亡。救援工作正在进行中。几小时后，被指控参与抓捕尼古拉斯·马杜罗行动的特种部队士兵将出庭受审。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Gannon Ken Van Dyke is due for a status hearing in Manhattan federal court. Prosecutors are alleging that he made more than $400,000 on prediction markets by making trades related to Venezuela before that January raid to capture Maduro. In a cage fight on President Trump's birthday this weekend is in danger of being knocked out. Two Virginia residents have filed a lawsuit to stop the UFC fight at the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 甘农·肯·范·戴克定于在曼哈顿联邦法院举行状态听证会。检察官指控他在1月突袭抓捕马杜罗之前，通过委内瑞拉相关交易在预测市场上获利超过40万美元。本周末在特朗普总统生日当天举行的一场笼斗比赛面临被取消的危险。两名弗吉尼亚州居民提起诉讼，要求停止在白宫举行的UFC比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The suit argues that the claw, built on the White House grounds was not approved by Congress or an environmental review. It also cited that UFC is selling VIP packages of more than a million dollars. And they argue that due to Trump's stock in UFC, he would profit off the fight. The fight is scheduled for this Saturday. And our breaking news coverage continues next. CNN is on the ground in Tehran with their new warning to Israel this morning as more missiles fly.
paragraphs.cn: 诉讼称，建在白宫场地上的这个笼子未经国会批准或环境审查。诉讼还指出，UFC正在销售超过一百万美元的VIP套餐。他们辩称，由于特朗普持有UFC股份，他将从这场比赛中获利。比赛定于本周六举行。我们的突发新闻持续报道。CNN在德黑兰现场报道伊朗今早对以色列的新警告，与此同时更多导弹飞过。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Plus, six days after the elections, the reality TV villain hoping to be the hero for L.A., could Spencer Pratt's bid for mayor be crumbling? var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 此外，选举六天后，这位真人秀反派希望成为洛杉矶的英雄，斯宾塞·普拉特的市长竞选是否会失败？

paragraphs.speaker: Announcer
paragraphs.en: This is CNN breaking news.
paragraphs.cn: 这里是CNN突发新闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: We are covering this breaking news this morning. Tensions flaring once again in the Middle East as Iran and Israel trade missile attacks. Iran state media released these images showing missiles being fired toward Israel. This was just a few hours ago. Israeli defense forces saying they are intercepting the attacks. Now the fighting this morning represents the worst escalation since the ceasefire went into effect in April.
paragraphs.cn: 今早我们正在报道这则突发新闻。中东局势再次紧张，伊朗和以色列互相发动导弹袭击。伊朗国家媒体发布了这些显示导弹射向以色列的图像。就在几小时前。以色列国防军表示正在拦截袭击。今早的战斗是自4月停火生效以来最严重的升级。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: The two countries started exchanging missile attacks early Sunday, which then carried on into Monday. Iranian officials say the new attacks were in response to Israel's ongoing strikes against Lebanon. EBRAHIM ZOLFAQARI, IRGC SPOKESPERSON (through translator): The Zionist Army must stop its attacks on southern Lebanon in the suburbs.
paragraphs.cn: 两国从周日清晨开始互相发动导弹袭击，一直持续到周一。伊朗官员表示，新的袭击是对以色列持续打击黎巴嫩的回应。伊朗革命卫队发言人埃布拉希姆·佐尔法加里（通过翻译）说："犹太复国主义军队必须停止对黎巴嫩南部郊区的攻击。"

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: And if it expands its attacks on that region or responds to Iran's actions it will face more crushing and regrettable blows and destructive attacks will begin against the regime and its supporters.
paragraphs.cn: "如果它扩大对该地区的攻击或回应伊朗的行动，它将面临更沉重和遗憾的打击，针对该政权及其支持者的毁灭性攻击将开始。"

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: CNN senior international correspondent Fred Pleitgen also filed this report from Tehran. We want to note that CNN operates in Iran with permission of the government but maintains full editorial control of our reporting. (BEGIN VIDEOTAPE)
paragraphs.cn: CNN高级国际记者弗雷德·普莱特根也从德黑兰发回了报道。我们注意到，CNN在伊朗经政府许可运营，但保持对我们报道的完全编辑控制。（开始录像带）

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: On our rooftop position here overlooking Iran's capital Tehran we have been able to hear a couple of very loud thuds that could be explosions, and then also some smaller ones in pretty close succession that might have been anti-aircraft fire. Now that's not confirmed. It's not exactly clear what that is. However, we do know that the Israelis say that they have been striking targets, as they put in, in Iran's capital Tehran but also on other Iranian cities like, for instance, Isfahan and Tabriz.
paragraphs.cn: 在我们俯瞰伊朗首都德黑兰的屋顶位置上，我们听到了几声非常响亮的砰声，可能是爆炸声，然后还有一些较小的声音，间隔很近，可能是防空火力。这尚未得到证实。目前还不清楚那是什么。然而，我们知道以色列人表示他们一直在打击目标，正如他们所说，在伊朗首都德黑兰以及其他伊朗城市，例如伊斯法罕和大不里士。

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: That's also confirmed by the Iranians. And the Iranians also say and the Israelis also confirm that Israel has hit a petrochemical facility in the south of Iran. It's very close to the Iraqi border and very close to the city of Basrah. The Iranians are saying that the staff of that petrochemical facility has been ordered to evacuate that facility. And the Iranians are also saying that they are hitting back in a major way.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗人也证实了这一点。伊朗人还说，以色列人也证实，以色列袭击了伊朗南部的一个石化设施。该设施非常靠近伊拉克边境，非常靠近巴士拉市。伊朗人表示，该石化设施的工作人员已被命令撤离。伊朗人还表示，他们正在以重大方式进行反击。

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: They say in retaliation for the attack on that petrochemical facility that they launched ballistic missiles towards a similar facility, they put it, in the Israeli city of Haifa. The Iranians also earlier saying that there had been a wave of Iranian ballistic missile attacks targeting two Israeli air bases and specifically, radar installations affiliated with those air bases.
paragraphs.cn: 他们表示，为报复对那家石化设施的袭击，他们向以色列城市海法的一处类似设施发射了弹道导弹。伊朗方面早些时候还称，已有一波伊朗弹道导弹袭击了以色列的两个空军基地，特别是与这些基地相关的雷达设施。

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: The Iranians are saying right now their missile forces and generally their forces are in a state of complete readiness, and they are also warning the Israelis to stop their military action. Of course, all of this coming as the ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran continues to very much hang in the balance. Iran and the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗方面目前表示，其导弹部队及整体部队已处于完全戒备状态，并警告以色列停止军事行动。当然，这一切都发生在美国与伊朗之间的停火协议仍然悬而未决之际。伊朗和美国

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: saying that they are trying to work towards a memorandum of understanding to end the conflict between Iran and the United States but also to then move towards broader peace talks aimed at achieving a larger peace agreement. The Iranians are saying those talks are in a difficult state. They want some of their frozen assets to be unfrozen immediately.
paragraphs.cn: 表示，他们正努力达成一份谅解备忘录，以结束伊朗与美国之间的冲突，并进而推动更广泛的和平谈判，旨在达成一项更大的和平协议。伊朗方面称，这些谈判处于困难状态。他们希望部分被冻结的资产能立即解冻。

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: And both sides are acknowledging, of course, right now, that especially with these attacks going on it certainly seems as though that ceasefire and the general situation right now very volatile. Fred Pleitgen, CNN, Tehran. (END VIDEOTAPE)
paragraphs.cn: 当然，双方都承认，尤其是随着这些袭击的持续，停火协议和整体局势目前看来非常不稳定。CNN记者弗雷德·普莱特根，德黑兰报道。（录像结束）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Once again, Fred Pleitgen talking to us from Iran. Now I want to bring in Avi Mayer. He's the founder of the Jerusalem Journal. He joins us live from Israel where missile sirens have been active throughout the day. So first, Avi, can you tell us at this time of day there what's happening where you are? AVI MAYER, FOUNDER, JERUSALEM JOURNAL (via Webex by Cisco): Well Audie, good morning. I'm actually here in suburban Maryland, not too far from you.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。再次感谢弗雷德·普莱特根从伊朗发回的报道。现在我想连线阿维·迈耶，他是《耶路撒冷日报》的创始人。他从以色列加入我们的直播，那里导弹警报整天都在响。首先，阿维，你能告诉我们你那里现在的情况吗？阿维·迈耶，《耶路撒冷日报》创始人（通过思科网真）：嗯，奥蒂，早上好。实际上我在马里兰郊区，离你不远。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But throughout the night my phone has been buzzing with notifications of missiles falling in locations throughout Israel. Millions of Israeli families have spent the entire night in bomb shelters, which is a condition with which we are now long familiar. And in many respects, this is not much of a surprise.
paragraphs.cn: 但整个晚上，我的手机一直响个不停，通知以色列各地有导弹落下。数百万以色列家庭整夜待在防空洞里，这种情况我们已经习以为常。从很多方面来看，这并不令人意外。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: To most of us in Israel we have enjoyed some period of calm over the past two months, but the residents of northern Israel will be asking what ceasefire? There has been ongoing missile and drone fire by Hezbollah towards northern Israel for the entirety of this period. Fifteen Israeli personnel have been killed by Israeli -- by Iranian-backed Hezbollah drone and missile strikes. That is more than the entirety of the 13 U.S. personnel killed in the war so far.
paragraphs.cn: 对我们以色列大多数人来说，过去两个月我们享受了一段平静期，但以色列北部的居民会问：什么停火？在此期间，真主党一直向以色列北部发射导弹和无人机。已有15名以色列人员被伊朗支持的真主党无人机和导弹袭击打死。这比迄今为止在战争中丧生的13名美国人员还要多。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And so in some respects this is not much of a surprise, but it is, of course, a dramatic escalation by the Islamic regime.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，从某些方面来说，这并不令人意外，但这当然是伊斯兰政权的重大升级。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Uh, can you talk about that idea of escalation? We were talking with military expert Cedric Leighton earlier about why Iran retaliated on this -- in regard to this attack on Lebanon, on Hezbollah, in Dahiya. That they treated that as a red line of some kind and escalated as a result. How is this being talked about by Israel? Do they see it as deterrent? Was it just another strike in the back-and-forth or was there something qualitatively different about that target?
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你能谈谈这种升级的概念吗？我们之前与军事专家塞德里克·莱顿讨论过，为什么伊朗要对黎巴嫩、真主党在达希耶的袭击进行报复。他们认为那是一条红线，并因此升级了行动。以色列方面是如何谈论此事的？他们视其为威慑吗？这只是一次你来我往的打击，还是那个目标有本质上的不同？

paragraphs.speaker: Mayer
paragraphs.en: Well, it's important to note that Dahiya is a suburb of Beirut that holds the headquarters of Hezbollah. Essentially, a Hezbollah stronghold. And what happened on Sunday over the weekend is that Israel finally struck that stronghold after months of ongoing missile and drone fire targeting northern Israel and injured hundreds of thousands of Israelis. And the Iranians decided that was going to be their red line.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，值得注意的是，达希耶是贝鲁特的一个郊区，是真主党总部所在地，基本上是真主党的据点。上周末周日发生的事件是，在数月来持续针对以色列北部的导弹和无人机袭击导致数十万以色列人受伤后，以色列终于打击了那个据点。而伊朗决定将其视为红线。

paragraphs.speaker: Mayer
paragraphs.en: Now again, it's very -- to be very clear, there has not been a ceasefire in effect in the north of Israel. It has been bombarded by Iran through its proxy, Hezbollah, over the course of this entire period by hundreds of drone and missile attacks. And this was Israel finally responding and saying enough is enough.
paragraphs.cn: 再次强调，需要明确的是，以色列北部并未实现停火。在此期间，伊朗通过其代理人真主党发动了数百次无人机和导弹袭击，对以色列进行轰炸。而以色列最终做出回应，表示受够了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Um, what is the relationship -- how is it talked about in terms of how Trump is talking publicly and the reporting about his frustrations with Netanyahu?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，特朗普公开表态的方式，以及有关他对内塔尼亚胡感到沮丧的报道，这两者之间是什么关系？

paragraphs.speaker: Mayer
paragraphs.en: Well, we understand that there have been several testy conversations between the two, but both have stated openly that they are aligned on the overall goals here. The goals being, of course, denying Iran the capacity to develop a nuclear weapon and severely degrading its military capabilities. We did hear President Trump say yesterday that it was -- he was calling on Israel not to retaliate.
paragraphs.cn: 我们了解到，两人之间有过几次激烈的对话，但双方都公开表示，他们在总体目标上是一致的。这些目标当然包括阻止伊朗发展核武器的能力，并严重削弱其军事能力。我们确实听到特朗普总统昨天表示，他呼吁以色列不要报复。

paragraphs.speaker: Mayer
paragraphs.en: But we're hearing from Israeli officials that the Israel response to the Iranian missile fire was fully coordinated with the United States, and the U.S. has been engaged in defending Israeli skies. So I think we're seeing certain messaging happening publicly but more happening behind the scenes.
paragraphs.cn: 但我们从以色列官员那里得知，以色列对伊朗导弹袭击的回应是与美国充分协调的，而美国一直参与保卫以色列领空。所以我认为，我们看到了一些公开的信息传递，但更多的事情发生在幕后。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Just so we're clear, again, comparing to the military notes from earlier, is it your sense that these are independent strikes Israel is doing or that this is happening with more than U.S. support? That it's happening with material backing.
paragraphs.cn: 为了明确起见，再次与之前的军事信息进行比较，您认为这些是以色列独立进行的打击，还是得到了美国不仅仅是支持？即得到了物质支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Mayer
paragraphs.en: As of right now, what we know is that these are Israeli airstrikes carried out by Israeli aircraft and other armaments but with full coordination with the United States. Meaning as far as we know right now, the U.S. is not actively engaged in offensive activity in Iran. But we have heard from various reports that the U.S. and perhaps other countries in the region have been involved in air defense, meaning preventing those missiles from reaching Israel -- reaching their targets as has happened throughout this war.
paragraphs.cn: 截至目前，我们所知道的是，这些是以色列飞机和其他武器装备进行的空袭，但与美国进行了充分协调。这意味着就目前所知，美国并未积极参与对伊朗的进攻行动。但我们从各种报道中得知，美国以及该地区其他国家可能参与了防空，即阻止那些导弹到达以色列 -- -- 阻止它们击中目标，正如这场战争中一直发生的那样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. That's Avi Mayer speaking to us here in Maryland. Thanks so much. And next on CNN THIS MORNING, our breaking news coverage continues. Yemen's Houthi rebels get in on the action targeting Israel. Are these latest escalations bound to expand? Plus --
paragraphs.cn: 好的。这是Avi Mayer在马里兰州接受我们的采访。非常感谢。接下来在CNN今晨节目中，我们将继续带来突发新闻的报道。也门的胡塞武装也加入了针对以色列的行动。这些最新的升级是否会扩大？此外 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: He has taken away the attention. We don't need that negativity. We need nothing by positive vibes.
paragraphs.cn: 他抢走了注意力。我们不需要那种消极情绪。我们只需要积极的气氛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Tonight, game three of the NBA final between the Knicks and the Spurs, but with President Trump in attendance some fans worry it's going to take away from the game. (COMMERCIAL)
paragraphs.cn: 今晚，尼克斯队与马刺队的NBA总决赛第三场即将开打，但特朗普总统的出席让一些球迷担心这会抢走比赛的风头。（广告）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Back to our breaking news as strikes are escalating between Israel and Iran. Now we're learning about another player in these latest attacks. Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen claiming responsibility for launching a missile towards Israel -- a missile Israeli forces say they intercepted. But the warning goes further. The Houthis are now declaring that they call a total ban on Israeli-linked shipping in the Red Sea, threatening to target vessels and potentially disrupt another critical global trade route.
paragraphs.cn: 回到我们的突发新闻，以色列和伊朗之间的袭击正在升级。现在我们了解到这些最新袭击中的另一个参与者。伊朗支持的也门胡塞武装声称向以色列发射了一枚导弹 -- -- 以色列军方称他们拦截了这枚导弹。但警告更进一步。胡塞武装现在宣布，他们呼吁全面禁止与以色列有关的航运通过红海，威胁要瞄准船只，并可能扰乱另一条关键的全球贸易路线。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: CNN's Paula Hancocks is live from Abu Dhabi for us. Can you help us understand the timeline here of when Houthis launched this strike with this back-and-forth between Iran and Israel?
paragraphs.cn: CNN的宝拉·汉考克斯正在阿布扎比为我们进行现场报道。您能帮我们梳理一下胡塞武装发动这次袭击的时间线，以及伊朗和以色列之间的来回交锋吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: So Audie, this is information coming to us from the Houthis this morning -- this Monday morning -- and it follows what we have seen as the largest escalation in strikes and tensions between Israel and Iran since that ceasefire was signed back in April. Now the Houthis are a proxy of Iran. They have in the past been believed to be operating fairly autonomously but they certainly have got involved on a number of occasions in support of Iran, specifically firing rockets and missiles towards Israel.
paragraphs.cn: 奥蒂，这是胡塞武装今天早上 -- -- 这个周一早上 -- -- 向我们提供的信息，而在此之前，我们看到自四月份签署停火协议以来，以色列和伊朗之间的袭击和紧张局势出现了最大规模的升级。胡塞武装是伊朗的代理人。过去，他们被认为行动相当自主，但确实多次参与支持伊朗的行动，特别是向以色列发射火箭弹和导弹。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: So what we've heard from this morning is that they say that they had a missile barrage targeting sensitive Israeli enemy targets. Israel says that they did intercept a projectile from Yemen itself. But, of course, the second part of this is that the Houthi rebels are threatening to disrupt global commerce even more than it is already. There is a very key maritime area, the Bab al-Mandab Strait, which is an area that runs into the Red Sea.
paragraphs.cn: 所以今天早上我们听到的是，他们声称对以色列的敏感敌方目标发动了一轮导弹齐射。以色列表示，他们确实拦截了一枚来自也门的射弹。但当然，第二部分是胡塞武装威胁要进一步扰乱全球商业，甚至比现在更严重。有一个非常关键的海域 -- -- 曼德海峡，这是通往红海的区域。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: They have threatened to disrupt traffic going through that area, specifically saying that Israeli vessels will not be allowed to navigate into the Red Sea. Now they have done this in the past and they did not restrict themselves to Israeli vessels even though they said that was their intention. We have seen that they have fired upon vessels over recent years causing devastation, really, in that very critical water chokepoint.
paragraphs.cn: 他们威胁要扰乱通过该区域的交通，特别表示不允许以色列船只进入红海。过去他们曾这样做过，而且尽管他们声称只针对以色列船只，但实际上并未限制自己。我们看到他们在过去几年里向船只开火，确实在那个关键的水道瓶颈造成了破坏。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: And it comes at the same time as we are seeing very little getting through the Strait of Hormuz as well, further to the north. So if there was disruption for both of these key waterways then it would cause chaos in the global economy -- even more than we have seen from the Strait of Hormuz. So certainly, this is being taken seriously. Israel says that it is able to intercept missiles from Yemen -- from these Houthi rebels.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，我们看到霍尔木兹海峡的通行量也非常少，更靠北一些。因此，如果这两个关键水道都受到干扰，将会给全球经济带来混乱 -- -- 甚至比我们从霍尔木兹海峡看到的更严重。所以，这当然被认真对待。以色列表示，它能够拦截来自也门 -- -- 这些胡塞武装分子 -- -- 的导弹。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: But, of course, in this escalation it does show another front potentially opening up in the midst of this very shaking ceasefire -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 但当然，在这次升级中，确实显示出在这个非常脆弱的停火协议期间，可能又开辟了一条新战线 -- -- 奥蒂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. That's Paula Hancocks speaking to us from Abu Dhabi. Now it is 46 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. Almost a week out, the votes are still being counted in L.A. Nithya Raman is pulling ahead of former reality TV star Spencer Pratt in the Los Angeles mayoral race. Raman, an L.A. City Council member, gained about 3,000 more votes than Pratt on Sunday. The two are fighting to see who will face off against incumbent Mayor Karen Bass in November.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。这是宝拉·汉考克斯从阿布扎比发回的报道。现在是整点过46分。这是您的早间简报。将近一周过去了，洛杉矶的选票仍在统计中。妮提亚·拉曼在洛杉矶市长竞选中领先于前真人秀明星斯宾塞·普拉特。洛杉矶市议员拉曼在周日比普拉特多获得了约3000张选票。两人正在争夺与现任市长凯伦·巴斯在11月对决的机会。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right now, Chinese leader Xi Jinping is in North Korea for rare diplomatic talks with Kim Jong Un. He's expected to spend two days there. This is his first trip since 2019, and it's seen as an important opportunity to strengthen relations. And it's game three of the NBA Finals between the Knicks and Spurs. Thanks to President Trump's attendance there will be heavy security. Secret Service will deploy snipers, intelligence, and surveillance teams. The watch party outside Madison Square Garden has also been canceled.
paragraphs.cn: 目前，中国领导人习近平正在朝鲜与金正恩进行罕见的外交会谈。他预计将在那里停留两天。这是他自2019年以来的首次访问，被视为加强关系的重要机会。这是尼克斯队与马刺队之间的NBA总决赛第三场。由于特朗普总统出席，安保将非常严密。特勤局将部署狙击手、情报和监视小组。麦迪逊广场花园外的观赛派对也已取消。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Unfortunately, I think it's kind of a shame. I really would like it, you know, because we've been waiting for this for, like, how long? And, you know, a lot of people can't afford to get into the game. I'm one of those people. So I was actually looking forward to going to one of these watch parties.
paragraphs.cn: 不幸的是，我觉得这有点遗憾。我真的很想去，你知道，因为我们等了多久了？而且，很多人买不起门票。我就是其中之一。所以我本来很期待去参加这些观赛派对之一。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: This is a monumental day in New York City and you're going to cancel it? It's all right. We going to find a way, OK? If you guys know real New Yorkers, we going to find a way.
paragraphs.cn: 这是纽约市具有里程碑意义的一天，你们要取消它？没关系。我们会想办法的，好吗？如果你们了解真正的纽约人，我们会找到办法的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The Knicks are up by two against the Spurs. And next on CNN THIS MORNING, President Trump says he calls the shots and Israel will fall in line, but did Israel just prove him wrong? And in our next hour it's the final stretch in Maine where Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner is looking to stabilize his campaign days before the election.
paragraphs.cn: 尼克斯队领先马刺队两分。接下来在CNN今晨节目中，特朗普总统说他发号施令，以色列会服从，但以色列刚刚证明他错了吗？下一小时，在缅因州的最后冲刺阶段，民主党参议员候选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳正试图在选举前几天稳定他的竞选活动。

paragraphs.speaker: Graham Platner
paragraphs.en: It doesn't matter what you do, but you gotta do something because the moment we're in right now, it's going to require all of us. (COMMERCIAL)
paragraphs.cn: 你做什么并不重要，但你必须做点什么，因为我们所处的这个时刻需要我们所有人。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: This shows you where Iran is right now. They've always been a sponsor of terror, and they are continuing it here against our allies in Israel.
paragraphs.cn: 这向你展示了伊朗现在的处境。他们一直是恐怖主义的赞助者，现在他们继续针对我们在以色列的盟友这样做。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Suddenly for Iran, after all this time, to decide that they were going to attack Israel for Hezbollah's actions make no sense.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗在这么久之后突然决定要为真主党的行动攻击以色列，这毫无道理。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Lawmakers on both sides of the aisle weighing in on the renewed strikes between Iran and Israel. On Sunday, President Trump held a phone call with Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu after Iran launched ballistic missiles aimed at Israel. He urged Netanyahu not to retaliate, according to a U.S. official. Netanyahu went against Trump's urging and hit targets in Iran leaving the existing ceasefire vulnerable.
paragraphs.cn: 两党议员就伊朗和以色列之间重新爆发的打击行动发表看法。周日，在伊朗向以色列发射弹道导弹后，特朗普总统与以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡通了电话。据一位美国官员称，他敦促内塔尼亚胡不要报复。内塔尼亚胡违背了特朗普的敦促，打击了伊朗境内的目标，使现有的停火协议变得脆弱。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Trump has also boasted to the Financial Times Netanyahu "won't have a choice but to accept any deal the U.S. negotiates with Iran," declaring "I call the shots. Netanyahu does not call the shots." But Netanyahu has defied him, so President Trump is trying again. He just posted on Truth Social moments ago, "Israel and Iran must immediately stop 'shooting.'" Ambassador Alon Pinkas joins me now. He's the former Israeli consul general in New York.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普还向《金融时报》夸口说，内塔尼亚胡"除了接受美国与伊朗谈判达成的任何协议外别无选择"，并宣称"我发号施令。内塔尼亚胡不发号施令。"但内塔尼亚胡违抗了他，所以特朗普总统再次尝试。他刚刚在Truth Social上发帖说："以色列和伊朗必须立即停止'射击'。"前以色列驻纽约总领事阿隆·平卡斯大使现在加入我的节目。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: First, can I ask you about that Truth Social post? The president saying both Israel and Iran must stop. ALON PINKAS, FORMER ISRAELI CONSUL GENERAL IN NEW YORK (via Webex by Cisco): Yeah. Good morning, Audie. This is something you would expect from the president to say, you know -- the diplomatic lingo of show restraint, exercise caution, patience, and so on. It's clear that he -- President Trump has to make this statement.
paragraphs.cn: 首先，我能问一下关于那条Truth Social帖子吗？总统说以色列和伊朗都必须停止。前以色列驻纽约总领事阿隆·平卡斯（通过Webex by Cisco）：是的。早上好，奥迪。这是你会期望总统说的话，你知道 -- -- 那种外交辞令，比如保持克制、谨慎、耐心等等。很明显，他 -- -- 特朗普总统必须发表这样的声明。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But basically, what this statement is saying -- you know, the underlining -- the underlining logic of it is that this is not my war anymore -- I, President Trump. This is not my way anymore. This is between Israel and Iran and I'm not part of this. I'm pursuing negotiations to get a deal -- good deal, bad deal, mediocre deal. We could discuss this.
paragraphs.cn: 但基本上，这个声明在说 -- -- 你知道，其潜在逻辑是 -- -- 这不再是我的战争了 -- -- 我，特朗普总统。这不再是我的方式了。这是以色列和伊朗之间的事，我不参与其中。我正在寻求谈判达成协议 -- -- 好协议、坏协议、平庸的协议。我们可以讨论这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Pinkas
paragraphs.en: He's basically saying to Mr. Netanyahu, Audie, you're on your own. And he's basically saying to the Iranians well, you're not necessarily going to have a deal if you keep on shooting. But the bottom line is this -- I'm not involved in this.
paragraphs.cn: 他基本上是在对内塔尼亚胡说，奥迪，你靠自己了。他基本上是在对伊朗人说，如果你们继续射击，你们不一定能达成协议。但底线是 -- -- 我不参与其中。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can I ask you about that, though? What we're hearing about what he's saying on Sunday to Netanyahu is very different from what you're explaining. He is trying to say --
paragraphs.cn: 不过我能问一下这个吗？我们听到的他在周日对内塔尼亚胡说的话与你解释的非常不同。他试图说 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- I'm the one in the lead here. I think the viewers are looking and wondering if Israel is in the lead here.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 我才是这里的主导者。我认为观众们都在看，想知道以色列是否处于主导地位。

paragraphs.speaker: Pinkas
paragraphs.en: Yeah, it's a very good question and, you know, it's open for interpretation. Let me put it in context and be brief about this. Netanyahu and Trump have a different political calculus. Trump is saying I want to end this war, and I can rein in Israel, and I -- and I will tell them what to do and they will do what I tell them. And I call the shots, et cetera, et cetera. Netanyahu, on the other hand, has defied Trump three times in the last three weeks with breaking -- violating the ceasefire in Lebanon.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这是个很好的问题，而且你知道，这可以有不同的解读。让我简要地把它放在背景中说明。内塔尼亚胡和特朗普有着不同的政治考量。特朗普说我想结束这场战争，我能约束以色列，我 -- -- 我会告诉他们该做什么，他们会照我说的做。我说了算，等等等等。另一方面，内塔尼亚胡在过去三周里三次违抗特朗普，违反了黎巴嫩的停火协议。

paragraphs.speaker: Pinkas
paragraphs.en: With attacking the Dahiya, which is the corridor --
paragraphs.cn: 通过攻击达希耶，那是走廊 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Pinkas
paragraphs.en: -- the neighborhood -- the area in Beirut where Hezbollah mostly is centered -- despite Trump's warnings. And again, last night when Iran launched a barrage of missiles. Trump called on Netanyahu to show restraint -- exercise restraint and not retaliate, and three times Netanyahu defied him. Netanyahu is speaking to a domestic audience. He's got an election in two or three months, either in September or October -- three or four months.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 那个社区 -- -- 贝鲁特地区，真主党主要集中在那里 -- -- 尽管特朗普发出了警告。还有，昨晚伊朗发射了一连串导弹。特朗普呼吁内塔尼亚胡保持克制 -- -- 展现克制，不要报复，而内塔尼亚胡三次违抗了他。内塔尼亚胡是在对国内听众讲话。他两三个月后就要举行选举，要么在九月要么在十月 -- -- 三四个月后。

paragraphs.speaker: Pinkas
paragraphs.en: So Netanyahu wants to brag that I stood up to the American president and when it came to national security and when it came to the defense of Israel, I stood up. And only I could do this. OK, et cetera, et cetera, et cetera. What Mr. Trump is doing, Audie, in stating more than once that he calls the shots and Netanyahu will have no choice but to accept, he's talking not necessarily about the ceasefire but about a framework or a provisional memorandum type of deal with Iran, meaning that --
paragraphs.cn: 所以内塔尼亚胡想吹嘘说，我顶住了美国总统的压力，当涉及国家安全和以色列的防御时，我顶住了。只有我能做到。好吧，等等等等。特朗普先生所做的，Audie，是多次声明他说了算，内塔尼亚胡别无选择只能接受，他说的不一定是指停火，而是指与伊朗的一个框架或临时备忘录式的协议，意思是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So you -- is there a red line? Can I just interrupt for a second? Is there a red line or is --
paragraphs.cn: 那么你 -- -- 有没有红线？我能打断一下吗？有没有红线，还是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- the messaging from Israel, regardless of what -- Netanyahu can make any decision and the U.S. will --
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 来自以色列的信息，不管怎样 -- -- 内塔尼亚胡可以做出任何决定，而美国会 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- essentially follow with a security guarantee.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 基本上会以安全保证作为后续。

paragraphs.speaker: Pinkas
paragraphs.en: Right. Well, not necessarily because if I'm right and Trump is essentially saying, Audie, that Israel is on its own and I'm not involved in this, Israel cannot sustain a prolonged conflict with Iran in terms of munitions, missile interceptor, depletion rate, and so on. And what Trump is basically saying, I'm calling the shots here. And yeah, there is a red line. If the red line is that -- well, the red line is further escalation --
paragraphs.cn: 对。嗯，不一定，因为如果我是对的，特朗普基本上是在说，Audie，以色列靠自己，我不参与其中，以色列无法在与伊朗的长期冲突中维持弹药、导弹拦截器、消耗率等等。而特朗普基本上在说，我说了算。是的，有一条红线。如果红线是 -- -- 嗯，红线是进一步升级 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Pinkas
paragraphs.en: -- that could inadvertently involve the U.S. That, I think, for Trump is a red line. I also think -- this perhaps is for a different segment -- that Trump is dissatisfied, disillusioned, and livid at Mr. Netanyahu for the basic --
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 这可能会无意中牵扯到美国。我认为，对特朗普来说，那是一条红线。我还认为 -- -- 这也许是另一个话题 -- -- 特朗普对内塔尼亚胡感到不满、失望和愤怒，主要是因为 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So then can there be a ceasefire going forward if this continues between Israel and Iran's proxies, meaning obviously, Israel has wanted to keep Lebanon out of the conversation. Iran keeps trying to pull it back in. It feels --
paragraphs.cn: 那么，如果以色列和伊朗的代理人之间继续这样下去，还能有停火吗？显然，以色列一直想把黎巴嫩排除在对话之外，而伊朗则不断试图将其拉回。感觉 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- as though this ceasefire could be over. It feels as though the war could go longer because Israel's prerogatives are different. That's what you're describing.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 好像停火可能结束了。感觉战争可能会持续更久，因为以色列的优先事项不同。这就是你描述的情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Pinkas
paragraphs.en: Absolutely. You're absolutely right, Audie. Netanyahu has several vested interests in this. He needs to maintain a permanent state of war. He needs to -- he wants politically to distance himself from the calamity of October 7, 2023, and somehow turn it into a strategic triumph, which could only come through Iran. That failed in the war. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 完全正确。你说得对，Audie。内塔尼亚胡在此事中有多重既得利益。他需要维持永久战争状态。他需要 -- -- 他在政治上想与2023年10月7日的灾难保持距离，并试图将其转变为战略胜利，而这只能通过伊朗实现。但这一点在战争中失败了。

paragraphs.en: And somehow, turned it into a strategic triumph which could only come through Iran. That failed in the war. So, he needs to, at least, going into an election period, he needs to at least maintain a permanent state of war. In that respect, he's been failing. And Trump has been dragged into this, and the failure is on Trump, as well.
paragraphs.cn: 并且，以某种方式将其转变为战略胜利，而这只能通过伊朗实现。但这一点在战争中失败了。所以，他至少需要在进入选举时期时维持永久战争状态。在这方面，他一直失败。特朗普也被卷入其中，失败也在于特朗普。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's Ambassador Alon Pinkas. Thank you for your time. I appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: 以上是阿隆·平卡斯大使。感谢您的时间。我很感激。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. Good morning. Thank you for waking up with us. I'm Audie Cornish, and our breaking news coverage continues right now.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。早上好。感谢您与我们一同醒来。我是Audie Cornish，我们的突发新闻报道现在继续。

paragraphs.speaker: Announcer
paragraphs.en: This is CNN breaking news.
paragraphs.cn: 这里是CNN突发新闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, we are following the news out of the Middle East. That's where Israel and Iran are engaging in a barrage of fresh strikes. It is a major test for negotiations that President Trump said just yesterday were very close to being done. So, we're going to start with a new wave of Iranian missile strikes that were launched towards Israel. This is new video into CNN overnight, showing damage inside a home in the West Bank. Now, this follows Israeli strikes on an Iranian petrochemical plant.
paragraphs.cn: 我们正在关注中东的新闻。以色列和伊朗正在进行新一轮的猛烈打击。这对谈判来说是一次重大考验，而特朗普总统昨天还说谈判已接近完成。因此，我们将从伊朗向以色列发射的新一轮导弹袭击开始。这是CNN连夜获得的新视频，显示西岸一处房屋内的损坏。此前，以色列袭击了伊朗的一家石化厂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The IDF said it hit several targets at that complex. Now these retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump, who called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and told him to hold off on striking back.
paragraphs.cn: 以色列国防军表示，他们打击了该综合体的多个目标。这些报复性打击似乎无视了特朗普总统，他曾致电以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡，要求他暂缓反击。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson said Monday that the U.S., quote, "bears responsibility" for Israel's actions, going on to say, quote, "whatever happens in the region, the direct responsibility of the United States is established, and it will also bear responsibility for the consequences of any escalation. Now, just now on Truth Social, President Trump, just in the last 30 minutes, writing, quote, "Israel and Iran must immediately stop shooting." We've got live team coverage throughout the region.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗外交部发言人周一表示，美国对以色列的行动"负有责任"，并进一步表示，"无论该地区发生什么，美国的直接责任都已确立，美国还将对任何升级的后果负责。"就在刚才，特朗普总统在Truth Social上，在过去的30分钟内写道："以色列和伊朗必须立即停止射击。"我们在整个地区有现场团队报道。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We're going to start in Jerusalem with CNN's Oren Liebermann. Oren, can you give us the latest on the strike so far this morning?
paragraphs.cn: 我们将从耶路撒冷开始，连线CNN的奥伦·利伯曼。奥伦，你能告诉我们今早这次袭击的最新情况吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: Audie, we just got a briefing a short time ago from an Israeli military official who said that Israel is preparing for the possibility of several days of fighting. And beyond that, the potential for this to become a more prolonged conflict. And that's important, because it gives you a sense of -- of what Israel is preparing for. Meanwhile, Tehran has also said they're prepared for a week of conflict.
paragraphs.cn: 奥黛，我们不久前刚从一名以色列军方官员那里得到简报，他说以色列正在为可能持续数天的战斗做准备。除此之外，还有可能演变成更持久的冲突。这很重要，因为它让你了解以色列正在准备什么。与此同时，德黑兰也表示他们已准备好应对一周的冲突。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: So, both Israel and Iran are clearly signaling right now that they're in this for the long haul, if that's what this turns into. If it turns into a few days or more of fighting, then they are prepared for that. In fact, it seems the one person who isn't prepared for that is President Donald Trump, who is trying to force, through his social media, a deescalation on both sides, in that post on -- on Truth Social that you pointed out.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，以色列和伊朗现在都明确表示，如果事态发展成这样，他们准备打持久战。如果演变成几天或更长时间的战斗，他们已经做好了准备。事实上，似乎唯一没有准备好的人是唐纳德·特朗普总统，他正试图通过他的社交媒体迫使双方缓和局势，就像你在Truth Social上指出的那篇帖子一样。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: That Israeli military official says that, since the start of this, which was only about 13 or 14 hours ago or so, Iran has launched some 30 ballistic missiles at Israel in several barrages. Meanwhile, Israel has carried out its own set of strikes on Iran, targeting surface-to-air missiles, as well as that petrochemical facility that you pointed out. That's key, because that may mean that energy infrastructure is fair game for Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 那位以色列军方官员说，自大约13或14小时前开始以来，伊朗已经分几轮向以色列发射了约30枚弹道导弹。与此同时，以色列也对伊朗发动了一系列打击，目标是地对空导弹以及你指出的那个石化设施。这很关键，因为那可能意味着能源基础设施对伊朗来说是公平的游戏目标。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: We should keep an eye on -- on the Gulf region to see if Iran carries out strikes on energy infrastructure there. That would, of course, send oil markets and the -- the energy part of this into turmoil, as well, or at least has the potential to do so. So, that's a very key element to keep an eye on here. Meanwhile, the Houthis in Yemen have also launched at least two missiles at Israel.
paragraphs.cn: 我们应该关注海湾地区，看看伊朗是否会对那里的能源基础设施发动打击。这当然会使石油市场和能源部分陷入动荡，或者至少有可能如此。所以，这是需要密切关注的一个非常关键的因素。与此同时，也门的胡塞武装也向以色列发射了至少两枚导弹。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: So, right now, it seems like we are climbing the escalation ladder potentially quickly, even as Trump tries to impose a ceasefire here. He has done it on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu before when it comes to Gaza and Lebanon and even previous conflicts with Iran. And it seems like he is trying to do so once again.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，现在看起来我们可能正在迅速升级紧张局势，即使特朗普试图在这里强加停火。他以前在加沙和黎巴嫩问题上，甚至在之前与伊朗的冲突中，都对本雅明·内塔尼亚胡总理这样做过。看起来他正试图再次这样做。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You said it seems like he's trying to do so, going as far as saying that he calls the shots, saying that Netanyahu will have no choice but to accept a deal with Iran. How is this being telegraphed in Israel? Are people hearing the way Trump is trying to rein Netanyahu in? And what has the Israeli government talked about?
paragraphs.cn: 你说他似乎在试图这样做，甚至说他说了算，声称内塔尼亚胡别无选择，只能接受与伊朗的协议。这在以色列是如何传达的？人们听到特朗普试图约束内塔尼亚胡的方式了吗？以色列政府又谈论了什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: It's safe to assume that, if Trump posts something related to Israel on -- on his social media or makes a public statement about it, it gets circulated here very, very quickly. As of right now, we haven't seen any sort of public statement from Netanyahu openly saying that he's going to defy Trump. Quite the opposite. Netanyahu would almost certainly never make a statement like that. He is perhaps Trump's biggest international cheerleader.
paragraphs.cn: 可以假设，如果特朗普在他的社交媒体上发布与以色列相关的内容，或者就此发表公开声明，它很快就会在这里传播开来。截至目前，我们还没有看到内塔尼亚胡公开声明他将违抗特朗普。恰恰相反。内塔尼亚胡几乎肯定不会发表这样的声明。他也许是特朗普最大的国际啦啦队长。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: And it's -- and it's going to stay that way, because Netanyahu knows that he needs Trump's support. Back in the previous fighting that we saw end in early April, Israel believed that the fighting would continue until Trump decides that it will stop. And that's likely the same scenario right now, even as -- as we're only a few hours into this. This will continue. Israel will have some rope, some decision-making autonomy in how this plays out, until Trump puts his foot down. And that's what we're looking for here.
paragraphs.cn: 而且这种情况会持续下去，因为内塔尼亚胡知道，他需要特朗普的支持。在之前于4月初结束的战斗中，以色列认为战斗会持续到特朗普决定停止为止。现在很可能也是同样的情况，即使我们才刚开始几个小时。这将继续下去。以色列将有一定的自由度，在事态发展中有一定的决策自主权，直到特朗普踩下刹车。这就是我们正在关注的。

paragraphs.speaker: Liebermann
paragraphs.en: Trump has posted on -- on social media. He spoke with Netanyahu last night, although we don't have a real sense of -- of what was said there. But Trump tried to urge either a holding off of or a delay of -- of retaliation. Netanyahu, clearly because of domestic political pressure here and because of incoming Iranian ballistic missiles, felt an absolute need to respond. And the question now to watch is how much space will Trump give him to maneuver and to strike?
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普在社交媒体上发了帖子。他昨晚与内塔尼亚胡通了电话，尽管我们不清楚具体说了什么。但特朗普试图敦促推迟或暂缓报复。内塔尼亚胡显然因为国内政治压力以及伊朗弹道导弹的来袭，感到绝对有必要做出回应。现在要关注的问题是，特朗普会给他多少回旋和打击的空间？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that's Oren Liebermann speaking to us from Jerusalem. Thank you. And our breaking news coverage is going to continue next. As we talked about Israel and Iran trading new strikes, did President Trump violate a campaign promise by starting this war in the first place? He's going to explain why he did not. We'll have that next. Plus, a deadly quake hits in the Philippines. The urgent search for survivors.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，以上是奥伦·利伯曼从耶路撒冷发回的报道。谢谢。接下来我们将继续关注突发新闻。我们刚刚讨论了以色列和伊朗互相发动新一轮打击，那么特朗普总统首先发动这场战争是否违反了他的竞选承诺？他将解释为什么没有违反。稍后带来。此外，菲律宾发生致命地震，正在紧急搜寻幸存者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, we are following the latest developments out of the Middle East, where the ceasefire is teetering after Iran and Israel traded the worst strikes in months. Despite the renewed strikes, Trump says he did not violate one of his core campaign promises: no new wars. KRISTEN WELKER, HOST, NBC'S "MEET THE PRESS": One of your consistent campaign promises was no new wars, going all the way back to 2015. Did you break that promise to the American people?
paragraphs.cn: 我们正在关注中东的最新进展，伊朗和以色列互相发动数月来最严重的打击后，停火协议岌岌可危。尽管再次发生打击，特朗普表示他没有违反核心竞选承诺之一：不发动新战争。NBC《与媒体会面》主持人克里斯汀·韦尔克：你一贯的竞选承诺是不发动新战争，这可以追溯到2015年。你是否违背了对美国人民的承诺？

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: No. No. I had to stop a country, a very powerful, very dangerous country from having a nuclear weapon. because they'd use it. They'd blow up the world. They'd blow up the Middle East. They'd blow up Israel. They'd come here, they blow up Europe. It's America first. I'm doing our country a service. When you say I promised, I didn't promise anything. I don't like these endless wars. This is not an endless war.
paragraphs.cn: 没有。没有。我必须阻止一个非常强大、非常危险的国家拥有核武器。因为他们会使用它。他们会炸毁世界。他们会炸毁中东。他们会炸毁以色列。他们会来到这里，炸毁欧洲。这是美国优先。我在为我们的国家服务。当你说我承诺过，我没有承诺任何事。我不喜欢这些无休止的战争。这不是一场无休止的战争。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining me now in the group chat, Sara Fischer, CNN's senior media analyst and senior media reporter at Axios; Elena Schneider, national political reporter at "NOTUS"; and Seung Min Kim, CNN political analyst and White House reporter at the Associated Press. Now, this ended up being a pretty testy interview for a variety of reasons, but I want to talk about this part about no endless wars. Because we're in a moment where the regional conflict is escalating. We just don't know how far this is going to spread.
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入群聊的有CNN高级媒体分析师、Axios高级媒体记者萨拉·费舍尔；《NOTUS》全国政治记者埃琳娜·施奈德；CNN政治分析师、美联社白宫记者金成敏。这次采访因多种原因变得相当激烈，但我想谈谈关于无休止战争的部分。因为我们正处于地区冲突升级的时刻，我们不知道它会蔓延多远。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Is this a sensitivity for this White House? When Hegseth is asked, he often says something like, Well, we meant no stupid wars. And I think that's sort of eye in -- of the beholder.
paragraphs.cn: 这是白宫的敏感点吗？当赫格塞斯被问到时，他经常说类似"我们指的是愚蠢的战争"这样的话。我认为这有点见仁见智。

paragraphs.speaker: Seung Min Kim
paragraphs.en: Right, right. And when you ask Trump about it, especially, you know, in these gaggles or in the interview yesterday, he says, no endless wars. He says he's talking about, you know, extricating ourselves from conflicts such as Afghanistan.
paragraphs.cn: 对，对。当你问特朗普时，尤其是在这些记者会或昨天的采访中，他说没有无休止的战争。他说他指的是让我们摆脱像阿富汗这样的冲突。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And he says it's only been three months.
paragraphs.cn: 他说这才三个月。

paragraphs.speaker: Kim
paragraphs.en: Exactly, exactly. And you saw that kind of messaging, even, you know, even a couple of months ago. I remember when he was talking about, in his Oval Office address, about when -- around the time the war started, he's talking about all of these conflicts in the past, such as Vietnam. And that he's like, we're not talking about those types of conflicts. But the sensitivity's there. It's because he did make that promise of no new wars during the campaign.
paragraphs.cn: 没错，没错。你看到这种信息传递，甚至几个月前就有。我记得他在椭圆形办公室讲话时，大约在战争开始的时候，他谈到过去的冲突，比如越南。他说我们不是在谈论那种冲突。但敏感点确实存在，因为他在竞选期间确实做出了不发动新战争的承诺。

paragraphs.speaker: Kim
paragraphs.en: That's why this -- kind of this unusual coalition, especially younger voters, really rallied around this president.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是为什么这种不寻常的联盟，尤其是年轻选民，真正团结在这位总统周围。

paragraphs.speaker: Kim
paragraphs.en: Because he made that promise. And now he's in a conflict that is so much more difficult than just a simple kind of -- a more -- a more simple strike that he made last summer in Iran on those nuclear facilities. And he -- he's struggling to get out of it. And that's why -- that's where that sensitivity is coming from, because it's kind of out of his control.
paragraphs.cn: 因为他做出了那个承诺。现在他陷入了一场比去年夏天对伊朗核设施进行的简单打击要困难得多的冲突。他很难摆脱出来。这就是敏感点的来源，因为这有点超出他的控制。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And it's the getting out that Americans are most politically sensitive to. I was watching the Platner Sunday rally in Maine. He's obviously sort of in a very political fight because of the allegations against him. But he talked about war, and he talked about war as suffering, pain, violence. And he says, one of the reasons I want to go to the United States Senate is I want to be a strong voice to stand up against war. Can you talk about this movement? I've seen other veterans on the campaign trail.
paragraphs.cn: 而正是撤军问题让美国民众最为政治敏感。我观看了普拉特纳在缅因州周日的集会。由于针对他的指控，他显然陷入了一场激烈的政治斗争。但他谈到了战争，将战争描述为苦难、痛苦和暴力。他说，我想进入美国参议院的原因之一，就是希望成为反对战争的强大声音。你能谈谈这场运动吗？我在竞选活动中看到过其他退伍军人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This is a talking point. because they see it as a Trump vulnerability.
paragraphs.cn: 这是一个话题点。因为他们认为这是特朗普的软肋。

paragraphs.speaker: Sara Fischer
paragraphs.en: It is a Trump vulnerability. And it's especially fascinating, because if you take a look at midterm advertising, very little talks about foreign policy, Iran, or the war.
paragraphs.cn: 这确实是特朗普的软肋。而且特别引人入胜，因为如果你看看中期选举的广告，很少涉及外交政策、伊朗或战争。

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: It's not a kitchen table issue that typically impacts these types of races at the local level.
paragraphs.cn: 这不是一个通常会在地方层面影响这类竞选的餐桌议题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Although Vote Vets is doing a lot of spending. Like, one of the PACS. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 尽管"投票退伍军人"组织投入了大量资金。比如，其中一个政治行动委员会。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: Well, now -- now that we've gotten this engaged in the war, we're starting to see some of this trickle in. So, there's this -- an element of surprise that veterans are leaning into this, because it's not typically something that sways people. I think, however, if you are a veteran and I mentioned this last time, but I did a debate for my hometown in which my hometown congressional district, one of the candidates who won their primary as a veteran. You see it in for yourself by talking about this war.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，现在 -- -- 既然我们已经卷入了这场战争，我们开始看到一些影响逐渐显现。所以，退伍军人倾向于谈论这个，这有点令人惊讶，因为这通常不是影响选民的因素。然而，我认为，如果你是一名退伍军人 -- -- 我上次提到过 -- -- 我为我的家乡主持了一场辩论，在我的家乡国会选区，其中一位赢得初选的候选人就是退伍军人。你通过谈论这场战争就能亲身体会到这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: You can draw attention to your military experience, to your experience as a veteran, which does matter when you're trying to compare yourself to other people on the campaign trail --
paragraphs.cn: 你可以利用你的军事经历和退伍军人身份来吸引注意，这在竞选活动中与他人比较时确实很重要 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: -- who don't have that experience. So, I think that's just probably one of the reasons that they bring it up. It's not because they have a big platform around how they're going to handle this. It's just a reminder to people, hey, I have a == I have empathy with the people who we are sending out to battle. And if I were in a position of power, I wouldn't be putting them there.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 尤其是对于那些没有这种经历的人。所以，我认为这很可能只是他们提出这个话题的原因之一。并不是因为他们有一个关于如何处理这个问题的宏大平台。这只是提醒人们，嘿，我对那些被派往战场的人有同理心。如果我掌权，我不会把他们送到那里。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I would somehow be different.
paragraphs.cn: 我会有所不同。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You know, it's interesting, watching Trump also navigate the domestic politics of this where, on his right, there is a very strong, loud, public kind of MAGA wing that really felt important, that this pledge was important about no endless wars. They can't be happy right now. ELENA SCHNEIDER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": No, of course not. And look, I mean, we had a poll last week that came out that 7 in 10 Americans want America to get out of Iran as soon as possible.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，有趣的是，观察特朗普如何应对国内政治，在他的右翼，有一个非常强大、喧闹、公开的MAGA派系，他们认为这个承诺很重要，即关于没有无休止的战争。他们现在不可能高兴。埃琳娜·施奈德，《NOTUS》国家政治记者：不，当然不高兴。而且，你看，我们上周发布了一项民调，显示70%的美国人希望美国尽快撤出伊朗。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, that's not just Republicans.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这不仅仅是共和党人的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Schneider
paragraphs.en: That really speaks to the -- the breadth of the concern here. And it's not only being felt in Democratic primaries where it's not too far to then take that next step of, we don't want more wars. And this war is affecting your gas prices, which is a very easy tie-in to the affordability message that we know Democrats want to make in this moment. But Republicans, too, feel this. Marjorie Taylor Greene was at the front lines of saying that this was some broken promises here. And I think she speaks for --
paragraphs.cn: 这确实说明了 -- -- 担忧的广泛性。不仅在民主党初选中能感受到，而且离下一步 -- -- 我们不想要更多战争 -- -- 并不遥远。这场战争正在影响你的油价，这与我们知道的民主党此刻想要传达的可负担性信息很容易联系起来。但共和党人也感受到了这一点。玛乔丽·泰勒·格林站在最前线，说这里有一些未兑现的承诺。我认为她代表了 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's what's interesting to me. They don't just talk about gas prices.
paragraphs.cn: 这对我来说很有趣。他们不仅仅谈论油价。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: They specifically talk about Trump, his relationship with Netanyahu, and their concerns about where the U.S. priority is. Is it abroad or is it at home?
paragraphs.cn: 他们特别谈论特朗普，他与内塔尼亚胡的关系，以及他们对美国优先事项的担忧。是国外还是国内？

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: Because it was a very sticky, very effective message for him. I think it really mattered to a lot of young people that he was making these kinds of promises. Because remember, at the time Joe Biden was tied -- you know, at the time the Biden administration was tied up in Ukraine. And the idea was, why can't we focus at home? And that was part of a key plank of what he was able to do in winning those younger voters by -- by margins that we had not seen Republicans do in quite some time.
paragraphs.cn: 因为对他来说，这是一个非常有效且深入人心的信息。我认为他做出这类承诺对很多年轻人来说确实很重要。因为请记住，当时乔·拜登被 -- -- 你知道，当时拜登政府被乌克兰问题缠身。而想法是，为什么我们不能专注于国内？这是他能够以相当长一段时间以来共和党未曾见过的优势赢得年轻选民的关键部分。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And ironically, I remember a time -- maybe you remember this in the media. There were these moments where people would say, well, Kamala Harris is going to drag you into a war with Iran. There was this concern that -- do you remember this?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。讽刺的是，我记得有一段时间 -- -- 也许你记得媒体上这样报道过。有些时刻人们会说，卡玛拉·哈里斯会把你拖入与伊朗的战争。当时有这样的担忧 -- -- 你记得吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The sort of connection being made between the Biden and Harris stance on Israel at that time, which was causing a lot of trouble in its primaries and with huge parts of its base. And now, it feels like, basically, Trump's in the same position with a part of his base.
paragraphs.cn: 当时拜登和哈里斯在以色列问题上的立场被联系在一起，这在其初选中和大部分基本盘中引起了很大麻烦。而现在，感觉基本上特朗普也处于同样的位置，面对他的一部分基本盘。

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: Yes. And going back to what you were saying on the Ukraine war, the big tension there was that why are we footing the bill to defend Ukraine? Well, it's a very similar situation here. You're asking Congress to approve billions of extra dollars for this war. Why is the U.S. footing the bill to fight Israel's war? One could make the argument that both are a national security issue for the
paragraphs.cn: 是的。回到你之前说的乌克兰战争，那里的主要紧张点是为什么我们要为保卫乌克兰买单？嗯，这里的情况非常相似。你要求国会批准数十亿美元的额外资金用于这场战争。为什么美国要为以色列的战争买单？有人可能会争辩说，两者都是美国的国家安全问题，

paragraphs.speaker: U.S.
paragraphs.en: to defend our allies. However, in the instance of Ukraine, Trump was very adamant that we shouldn't be putting forth a penny. In this situation, he feels much more comfortable asking Congress to appropriate this funds.
paragraphs.cn: 为了保卫我们的盟友。然而，在乌克兰的问题上，特朗普非常坚决地认为我们不应该出一分钱。在这种情况下，他更愿意要求国会拨出这笔资金。

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: Yes, for many pennies.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，很多钱。

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: And so, I think this is where there's the break in the MAGA base is, in their point of view, there should be the same scrutiny towards spending here as there was there. But there clearly is not.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我认为这就是MAGA阵营出现分歧的地方，在他们看来，对这里的支出应该像对那里一样严格审查。但显然并非如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, you guys, stay with me. We're going to bring in more folks to help us make sense of this, this morning. And our breaking news coverage continues after the break. We're going to talk about from LEGO propaganda to messages on missiles. Is Iran getting better at the information war? Plus, Trump's cage fight just days away. Could it get knocked out before round one? In the meantime, I want to say good morning to New York City, because, of course, tonight is game three of the NBA finals.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，各位，请继续关注。今天早上我们将邀请更多嘉宾来帮助我们理解这一切。我们的突发新闻报道将在广告后继续。我们将讨论从乐高宣传到导弹上的信息。伊朗是否在信息战中变得更擅长？另外，特朗普的笼斗比赛就在几天后。它会在第一轮之前就被取消吗？与此同时，我想对纽约市说声早安，因为今晚当然是NBA总决赛的第三场。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's 22 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. Democratic Senate primary candidate Graham Platner held an event in Maine in front of an enthusiastic and cheering crowd just two days before the primary. And he declared he is, quote, very much just some random guy from Sullivan, Maine. GRAHAM PLATNER (D),
paragraphs.cn: 现在是22分钟过整点。这是你的早间新闻摘要。民主党参议院初选候选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳在初选前两天在缅因州举行了一场活动，现场有热情欢呼的人群。他宣称自己，引用原话，"非常只是来自缅因州沙利文的一个普通人"。格雷厄姆·普拉特纳（民主党）：

paragraphs.speaker: Maine Senatorial Candidate
paragraphs.en: In a moment like this in history, if we can show that a regular person from the regular world, who simply comes out with a message saying that we all have to work together to push back against all of the structures of power that have been exploiting and oppressing us for generations. If we show that that works, we get to show this country the way forward.
paragraphs.cn: 在这样一个历史时刻，如果我们能证明一个来自普通世界的普通人，只是站出来说我们必须共同努力，抵制那些世代剥削和压迫我们的权力结构，如果我们证明这行得通，我们就能向这个国家展示前进的道路。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, Platner is still favored to win Tuesday's primary, even after "The New York Times" published a report from three of his ex- girlfriends who described, quote, "volatile and toxic relationships." In one account, physical intimidation, which he denies. A deadly 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck in the Philippines. Video from the area showing you right here. Massive damage, including crumbling buildings. Several people were killed. Rescues continue. And in a few hours, the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，普拉特纳仍然有望赢得周二的初选，即使《纽约时报》发表了一篇报道，其中他的三位前女友描述了"动荡且有毒的关系"。其中一则描述涉及身体恐吓，他对此予以否认。菲律宾发生了一场致命的7.8级地震。来自该地区的视频显示，这里出现了巨大破坏，包括建筑物倒塌。数人死亡。救援工作仍在继续。几小时后，美国

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Special Forces soldier charged with betting on the raid to capture Nicolas Maduro will appear in court. Gannon Ken Van Dyke is due for a status hearing in a Manhattan federal court. Prosecutors say that he made more than $400,000 on prediction markets by making trades related to Venezuela before the January raid to capture Maduro. And the cage fight on President Trump's birthday this weekend. Well, it's in danger of being knocked out.
paragraphs.cn: 一名被指控押注抓捕尼古拉斯·马杜罗突袭行动的特种部队士兵将出庭。加农·肯·范·戴克将在曼哈顿联邦法院参加状态听证会。检察官称，他在1月抓捕马杜罗的突袭行动前，通过预测市场进行与委内瑞拉相关的交易，赚取了超过40万美元。而本周末特朗普总统生日当天的笼斗比赛，则面临被取消的危险。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Two Virginia residents have filed a lawsuit to stop the UFC fight at the White House. The suit argues that the claw built on the White House lawns was not approved by Congress or an environmental review. It's also citing that UFC is selling VIP packages of more than $1 million, arguing that, due to Trump's stock in UFC, he would profit off the fight. It is scheduled for this Sunday. In the meantime, it's game three of the NBA finals tonight between the Knicks and Spurs.
paragraphs.cn: 两名弗吉尼亚州居民提起诉讼，要求停止在白宫举行的UFC比赛。诉讼称，白宫草坪上搭建的爪子未经国会批准或环境审查。诉讼还指出，UFC正在销售超过100万美元的VIP套餐，并认为由于特朗普持有UFC股票，他将从比赛中获利。比赛定于本周日举行。与此同时，今晚是NBA总决赛第三场，尼克斯队对阵马刺队。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Thanks to President Trump's attendance, there will be heavy security. Secret Service will deploy snipers, intelligence, and surveillance teams. With the Knicks up by two games, the watch party outside Madison Square Garden has also been canceled.
paragraphs.cn: 由于特朗普总统出席，安保将非常严密。特勤局将部署狙击手、情报和监视小组。由于尼克斯队领先两场，麦迪逊广场花园外的观赛派对也被取消。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Personally, I think it's kind of a shame. I really would like it, you know, because we've been waiting for this for, like, how long? And, you know, a lot of people can't afford to get into the game. I'm one of those people. So, I was actually looking forward to going to one of these watch parties.
paragraphs.cn: 就我个人而言，我觉得有点遗憾。我真的很想去，因为我们等这个等了多久了？而且，很多人买不起门票。我就是其中之一。所以，我原本很期待去参加这些观赛派对之一。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: This is a monumental day in New York City, and you're going to cancel it? It's all right. We're going to find a way. OK? If you guys know real New Yorkers, we're going to find a way.
paragraphs.cn: 这是纽约市具有里程碑意义的一天，你们却要取消它？没关系。我们会找到办法的。好吗？如果你们了解真正的纽约人，我们会找到办法的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I don't want to mess with New Yorkers. They finally get to see. I hadn't actually thought about it until I heard that little clip of someone being, like, some of us can't afford tickets.
paragraphs.cn: 我不想惹纽约人。他们终于能看到了。我其实没想过这事，直到我听到那段小片段，有人说，比如，我们有些人买不起票。

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: It's true. And by the way, live experiences are becoming so much more expensive. You saw people on social media saying it's cheaper to fly to San Antonio for some of those games as a New Yorker than it is to go down the street.
paragraphs.cn: 确实如此。而且，现场体验变得越来越贵。你在社交媒体上看到有人说，作为纽约人，飞往圣安东尼奥看某些比赛比在本地看还便宜。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Is Trump still the ultimate New Yorker? Like, is he popular there?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。特朗普还是那个终极纽约人吗？比如，他在那里受欢迎吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Kim
paragraphs.en: I mean, I -- I think it's fair to say that if there are loud boos tonight when he's in Madison Square Garden, I don't think any -- any of us would be surprised.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，我认为可以公平地说，如果他今晚在麦迪逊广场花园听到响亮的嘘声，我们谁都不会感到惊讶。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: yes. Because he's been at Knicks games plenty over the years.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。因为他多年来经常出现在尼克斯队的比赛中。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We're showing him here. But you know, let's just say.
paragraphs.cn: 我们这里展示了他。但你知道，我们就这么说吧。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: Well, he might be the ultimate sports president. You know, he keeps popping up at all of these big games. Right? He was at the Daytona 500. He went to the Super Bowl. He's been to the Ryder Cup. He went to the U.S. Open.
