I mean, I don't know if any other president has leaned on the -- the privileges of being able to attend some of these major games quite the way that he has during his presidencies.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，他可能是终极体育总统。你知道，他不断出现在这些大型比赛中。对吧？他去了代托纳500，他去了超级碗，他去了莱德杯，他去了美国公开赛。我的意思是，我不知道还有其他总统像他这样在任期内如此利用特权参加这些重大赛事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, well, shout-out to the New Yorkers who are not going to get to go to the watch party. I hope you all find a way. OK?
paragraphs.cn: 好的，那么向那些无法参加观赛派对的纽约人致敬。我希望你们都能找到办法。好吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, President Trump saying that he calls the shots and that Israel will fall in line. But did Netanyahu just prove him wrong? Plus, CNN is on the ground in Tehran with their new warning to Israel this morning as more missiles fly. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 接下来在CNN今晨节目中，特朗普总统说他发号施令，以色列会服从。但内塔尼亚胡刚刚证明他错了吗？另外，CNN在德黑兰现场报道，随着更多导弹发射，他们今早向以色列发出新警告。

vocabulary.excerpt: Israel and Iran trading missile attacks
vocabulary.exampleCn: 以色列和伊朗相互发动导弹袭击。

vocabulary.en: a ceasefire that is easily broken
vocabulary.excerpt: the fragile ceasefire survive this test
vocabulary.exampleCn: 脆弱的停火经受住了这次考验。

vocabulary.excerpt: those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump
vocabulary.exampleCn: 那些报复性打击似乎是在违抗特朗普总统。

vocabulary.excerpt: told him to hold off on striking back

vocabulary.en: a concentrated artillery or missile bombardment
vocabulary.excerpt: approximately three barrages in that first wave
vocabulary.exampleCn: 第一波大约有三轮齐射。

vocabulary.en: to stop or catch something in transit
vocabulary.excerpt: most of those have been intercepted

vocabulary.cn: 升级阶梯（冲突逐步升级的步骤）
vocabulary.en: a series of steps that lead to increased conflict
a person or group authorized to act on behalf of another
vocabulary.excerpt: the Houthis in Yemen; an Iranian proxy
vocabulary.exampleCn: 也门的胡塞武装，伊朗的代理人。

vocabulary.en: something used to gain an advantage in negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: using Lebanon as a chip -- bargaining chip
vocabulary.exampleCn: 把黎巴嫩当作谈判筹码。

vocabulary.en: likely to change suddenly and unpredictably
vocabulary.excerpt: over what was a very volatile weekend
vocabulary.exampleCn: 在一个非常动荡的周末。

vocabulary.en: a limit beyond which action will be taken
vocabulary.exampleCn: 那越过了以色列的红线。

vocabulary.excerpt: to try to rein in Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu
vocabulary.exampleCn: 试图约束总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡。

sentences.en: Israel says it has identified a wave of missiles launched from Iran.
sentences.cn: 以色列称已识别出一波从伊朗发射的导弹。
sentences.structure: 主句：Israel says + 宾语从句：it has identified a wave of missiles launched from Iran（过去分词短语作定语）
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句中过去分词短语 'launched from Iran' 修饰 'missiles'，表示被动。

sentences.en: Now, those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump, who called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and had told him to hold off on striking back.
sentences.cn: 现在，这些报复性打击似乎是在违抗特朗普总统，他打电话给以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡，并告诉他暂缓反击。
sentences.structure: 主句：those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump + 非限制性定语从句：who called... and had told...
sentences.analysis: 定语从句中 'who' 指代 President Trump，两个并列谓语 'called' 和 'had told'，'had told' 表示过去的过去。

sentences.en: According to Israel's military, most of those have been intercepted.
sentences.cn: 据以色列军方称，其中大多数已被拦截。
sentences.structure: 状语：According to Israel's military + 主句：most of those have been intercepted
sentences.analysis: 被动语态 'have been intercepted' 表示动作已完成。

sentences.en: It is possible that Trump essentially gave this the green light and said, yes, Israel has to respond in some fashion, but keep that response limited.
sentences.cn: 有可能特朗普基本上开了绿灯，说，是的，以色列必须以某种方式回应，但保持回应有限。
sentences.structure: 主句：It is possible + 主语从句：that Trump... said... + 直接引语：yes, Israel has to respond... but keep...
sentences.analysis: 主语从句中 'gave the green light' 是习语，意为'开绿灯'；直接引语中 'keep that response limited' 是祈使语气。

sentences.en: What's crucial here, Audie, is that what happens in Lebanon affects the U.S.-Iran ceasefire.
sentences.cn: 关键的是，Audie，黎巴嫩发生的事情会影响美伊停火。
sentences.structure: 主句：What's crucial here is + 表语从句：that what happens in Lebanon affects the U.S.-Iran ceasefire
sentences.analysis: 主语从句 'What's crucial here' 作主语；表语从句中又包含主语从句 'what happens in Lebanon'。

topics.title: 以色列与伊朗冲突升级
topics.content: 2026年6月8日，以色列和伊朗相互发动导弹袭击。伊朗瞄准以色列拉马特戴维空军基地，以色列打击伊朗石化厂和防空系统。这是自4月停火以来最严重的升级。冲突涉及代理人如胡塞武装和真主党。
topics.keywords: Israel · Iran · missile attacks · ceasefire · escalation

topics.title: 特朗普的角色与美以关系
topics.content: 美国总统特朗普曾要求以色列总理内塔尼亚胡暂缓报复，但以色列仍发动打击。分析认为特朗普可能默许有限回应。特朗普此前曾迫使以色列结束加沙、黎巴嫩和伊朗的战争，此次可能再次介入。
topics.keywords: Trump · Netanyahu · green light · U.S.-Israel relations

topics.title: 黎巴嫩与真主党问题
topics.content: 黎巴嫩总统约瑟夫·奥恩指责伊朗利用黎巴嫩作为谈判筹码。真主党是伊朗在黎巴嫩的代理人，其活动影响美伊停火。以色列在黎巴嫩南部打击真主党，导致超过3600人死亡。

topics.title: 胡塞武装与也门局势
topics.content: 也门胡塞武装作为伊朗代理人，向以色列发射导弹。胡塞武装威胁封锁曼德海峡，影响全球航运。该组织是伊朗在中东影响力的一部分，加剧地区紧张。

quiz.question: 在 'those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump' 中，'in defiance of' 最接近的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'in defiance of' 意为'无视、违抗'，与选项B相符。原文指以色列的打击违抗了特朗普的要求。

quiz.question: 短语 'hold off on' 在 'told him to hold off on striking back' 中的含义是？
quiz.explanation: 'hold off on' 意为'推迟、暂缓'，特朗普要求内塔尼亚胡暂缓反击。

quiz.question: 在 'using Lebanon as a chip -- bargaining chip' 中，'bargaining chip' 指的是？
quiz.explanation: 'bargaining chip' 是固定短语，意为'谈判筹码'，指在谈判中用来获取优势的东西。

quiz.question: 根据报道，以色列对伊朗的报复性打击包括哪些目标？
options: B. 石化厂和防空系统
quiz.explanation: 报道提到以色列打击了伊朗的石化厂和战略防御系统（防空系统），而非核设施或石油港口。

quiz.question: 黎巴嫩总统约瑟夫·奥恩对伊朗的主要批评是什么？
options: B. 伊朗利用黎巴嫩作为谈判筹码
quiz.explanation: 奥恩说：'They are using Lebanon as a chip -- bargaining chip in their negotiation with the United States.' 即伊朗把黎巴嫩当作谈判筹码。

quiz.question: 关于特朗普与内塔尼亚胡的通话，报道暗示了什么？
options: A. 特朗普明确禁止以色列报复
B. 特朗普可能默许了有限回应
C. 特朗普支持全面战争
quiz.explanation: 报道说'It is possible that Trump essentially gave this the green light... but keep that response limited'，暗示特朗普可能默许了有限回应。
